messages.content: You output JSON for task plans.

You are a senior project planner producing structured task plans.

Follow this JSON schema:
TaskPlan { originalPrompt: string; subtasks: Subtask[]; totalSubtasks: number; decompositionStrategy?: string }
Subtask { id: string (kebab-case), title: string, description: string (>=10 chars), status: one of pending|in_progress|completed|failed, dependencies: string[], estimatedComplexity: low|medium|high, successCriteria: string }
Constraints: 2-10 subtasks, actionable descriptions, include logical dependencies, avoid circular references, IDs unique.
Original prompt: Framework: Express, Styling: Tailwind CSS

Original request: create hello world app, where i can modify the text "hello world" colors, through a frontend ui
Clarifications:
- framework: Express
- styling: Tailwind CSS
Previous validation issues:
None
Respond ONLY with JSON matching the schema.

response:
{
  "originalPrompt": "Framework: Express, Styling: Tailwind CSS",
  "subtasks": [
    {
      "id": "project-init",
      "title": "Initialize project and dependencies",
      "description": "Create project directory, initialize npm, set up git, and install core dependencies (express, nodemon, tailwindcss, postcss, autoprefixer) and a simple build script.",
      "status": "in_progress",
      "dependencies": [],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "Repository created with package.json, git initialized, and required dependencies listed in package.json; npm scripts for dev and build exist."
    },
    {
      "id": "express-setup",
      "title": "Set up Express server and static serving",
      "description": "Create an Express server file that serves static assets from a public folder and provides a base route for index.html; include hot-reload dev script (nodemon) for development.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "project-init"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "Express server starts with npm run dev and serves index.html and static assets from the public directory on the configured port."
    },
    {
      "id": "tailwind-config",
      "title": "Configure Tailwind CSS build pipeline",
      "description": "Install and configure Tailwind via CLI/PostCSS: create tailwind.config.js, postcss.config.js (or use Tailwind CLI build), and a source CSS that includes Tailwind directives; set up npm build to output public/styles.css.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "project-init"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "Tailwind is configured and a compiled CSS file (public/styles.css) is generated that contains Tailwind utilities when the build script runs."
    },
    {
      "id": "frontend-static",
      "title": "Create base HTML and static asset structure",
      "description": "Add public/index.html, public/scripts.js and reference compiled Tailwind CSS; create folders for assets and ensure index.html contains a container for the editable 'hello world' text.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "express-setup",
        "tailwind-config"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "index.html loads in the browser, references public/styles.css and public/scripts.js, and displays the default 'hello world' text area."
    },
    {
      "id": "ui-components",
      "title": "Build frontend UI controls with Tailwind",
      "description": "Implement a small UI in index.html (and scripts.js) including a text input for the message, color pickers for text and background, and preview area styled with Tailwind classes for responsiveness.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "frontend-static",
        "tailwind-config"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "UI shows editable text input, color pickers, and a live preview that updates visually when inputs change (without backend integration)."
    },
    {
      "id": "api-endpoints",
      "title": "Implement Express API to get/save settings",
      "description": "Create lightweight API routes (e.g., GET /api/settings and POST /api/settings) that store the hello text and color choices in-memory (or JSON file) and return current settings.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "express-setup"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "API endpoints respond with JSON and accept updates; POST persists to in-memory store (or file) and GET returns the latest values."
    },
    {
      "id": "connect-frontend-backend",
      "title": "Connect UI to API and persist changes",
      "description": "Add frontend JS to fetch initial settings from GET /api/settings, apply them to the preview, and POST updates when the user changes text or colors; handle success/error feedback.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "ui-components",
        "api-endpoints"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "Changing inputs updates the preview and a save triggers a successful POST; reloading the page reflects saved settings from the backend."
    },
    {
      "id": "testing-and-validation",
      "title": "Test functionality and edge cases",
      "description": "Manually and minimally automate tests: verify server starts, static files serve, API responds correctly, UI updates preview, color values valid, and persistence works across reloads.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "connect-frontend-backend"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "All manual test cases pass: server serves UI, API returns saved settings, UI updates preview, and inputs are validated; basic automated tests (if created) pass."
    },
    {
      "id": "documentation",
      "title": "Write README and run instructions",
      "description": "Create README with setup, dev, and build instructions, describe the API endpoints and how to modify text/colors; include commands to run the project locally.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "project-init",
        "testing-and-validation"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "README contains clear steps to install, run, build, and test the app so another developer can reproduce the environment and use the UI to change text/colors."
    }
  ],
  "totalSubtasks": 9,
  "decompositionStrategy": "Break the project into environment setup, server configuration, CSS toolchain, static frontend, UI components, API, integration, testing, and documentation to ensure incremental, testable progress with clear dependencies."
}
